reasoning.typography: Generic sans-serif system with no discernible character — could be any agency site from 2018-2024. No typographic identity.
reasoning.hierarchy: Headline dominates correctly, but the repetitive card grid below undermines any sense of intentional progression. Adequate but predictable.
reasoning.distinctiveness: Utterly interchangeable — 'We solve complex problems through design & technology' could be Work & Co, Metalab, Clay, etc. Pure stencil copy.
reasoning.color: Red accent bar at top and scattered red links show some restraint, but the beige/coral/purple cards feel arbitrary rather than systematic.
reasoning.density: Safe, generous padding everywhere. Not confident density, just standard agency whitespace. 'Latest' and 'Updates' sections feel obligatory rather than intentional.
reasoning.voice: Pure stencil: hero statement + testimonial + 'Latest' + case study cards. Zero reflection of actual story or point of view. Could swap all content for another firm.

winning_patterns: Clean headline hierarchy with clear focus on main value prop
Restrained use of red accent in logo and links shows some discipline
anti_patterns: Generic 'We solve complex problems through design & technology' stencil headline — zero differentiation
Obligatory 'Latest' and 'Updates' sections with uniform card grids — template IA
No typographic personality — anonymous sans-serif like 1000 other agencies
'Part of Accenture Song' branding feels tacked on, undermines independence
Case study cards with identical padding/structure — assembly line layout
Cookie banner blocks 20% of viewport with generic compliance copy
